evidence_spans.span: The forward Euler method is applied to an initial value problem by advancing from the current approximation along the current vector-field slope. Its update is y_{n+1} = y_n + hF(t_n,y_n).
A first-order equation can be written as y'(t) = F(t,y(t)).

results.rationale: The Euler predictor is a step in Heun's method, which is an extension of the forward Euler method. The forward Euler method is applied to first-order equations, as evidenced by the update formula using F(t_n,y_n) from the first-order equation form.
